title: Voter support or MLAs' backing? Congress weighs its options in Kerala
content: Congress leadership is deliberating on the Kerala CM post, weighing intra-party support for KC Venugopal against public sentiment favoring VD Satheesan. A decision is expected by Sunday, with discussions involving key state leaders and central party chiefs in Delhi.

title: Kolkata Police unfollows Mamata on X; follows PM Modi, Amit Shah after BJP win
content: Kolkata Police's official X handle has shifted its following to prominent BJP leaders like PM Modi and Amit Shah, unfollowing Mamata Banerjee and Abhishek Banerjee. This digital change follows the BJP's assembly election victory and coincides with the withdrawal of additional security from TMC offices and residences.

title: No tap, no toilet, no power: The cracks within ST households
content: Telangana's development narrative is challenged by a stark reality: thousands lack basic amenities like toilets, tap water, and electricity. A recent survey reveals deep inequalities, particularly affecting Scheduled Tribes and Castes, highlighting persistent social and economic backwardness despite claims of progress. This disparity impacts health, dignity, and overall quality of life for many.

title: Busted! Virat Kohli's RCB match ticket turns out to be AI-generated
content: Lucknow Super Giants secured their maiden home win in IPL 2026 against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, but the match was marred by the discovery of fake AI-generated tickets. Police apprehended four individuals from Chhattisgarh for using AI tools and graphic software to create and sell counterfeit tickets outside the Ekana Stadium.

title: Blood-stained cloth stuffed in mouth: New details in retd IPS officer’s wife’s murder
content: A chilling case has emerged in Hyderabad, where Tanuja Ranjan, the spouse of a former IPS officer, was found dead in her home. Investigators suspect foul play by a domestic worker and an accomplice, who allegedly smothered her after restraining her. It appears that the motive behind this horrific act was theft, with significant valuables disappearing from the scene.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn; gives 540 workers $240m
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: 'You have 10 working days to voluntarily recognise unions': Google UK staff tells company
content: Google's DeepMind employees are demanding union recognition amid concerns over their AI technology's use by the US and Israeli militaries. Workers seek a commitment against developing AI weapons and surveillance tools, urging management to voluntarily recognize their unions or face legal action. The move highlights growing anxieties about AI's ethical implications and its potential for harm.

title: What will it take to free 1,600 stranded ships from Hormuz deep waters?
content: President Trump's "Project Freedom" to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz lasted only 48 hours, leaving thousands of vessels stranded. Despite US military guidance for two ships, the operation was paused due to ongoing dangers, with shipping executives unwilling to risk passage without a proven peace accord.

title: Will rupee continue its free fall against US dollar or recover?
content: The Indian rupee is facing significant pressure, weakening against the US dollar due to soaring crude oil prices driven by Middle East geopolitical tensions and persistent foreign fund outflows. Despite India's robust economic growth, the currency's slide continues as the Reserve Bank of India intervenes to curb volatility rather than target a specific exchange rate.

title: Midnapore gets ‘local’ CM: Contai cannot contain its pride as homeboy Suvendu takes top post
content: Suvendu Adhikari's ancestral home in Contai is now a focal point as he is set to become West Bengal's Chief Minister. This marks a significant moment for Midnapore district, which hasn't had a CM in five decades. Celebrations erupted in his hometown, with locals expressing pride and hope for renewed respect for the region.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to Trump Accounts, Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m to Texas University
content: Michael and Susan Dell are donating $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin to establish a new AI-focused medical center and research campus. This significant philanthropic gift, named UT Dell Medical Center, will integrate medicine, science, and computing, aiming to revolutionize healthcare through artificial intelligence.

title: Fuel price hike coming? Oil PSUs losing almost Rs 30,000cr per month
content: Soaring crude oil prices, averaging $105.4 a barrel, are straining oil companies' finances as they sell fuel below cost. Despite government excise duty cuts, losses are unsustainable. Experts suggest gradual price hikes and innovative strategies to manage the transition to renewables while supporting companies' investments and the macroeconomy.

title: From Garhwal Rifles to CDS - who is India’s new military chief NS Raja Subramani?
content: Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani has been appointed as the next Chief of Defence Staff, succeeding General Anil Chauhan. He will also serve as secretary of the Department of Military Affairs. Lt Gen Subramani brings extensive experience from various command and staff appointments, including leading formations in diverse operational environments and serving as Vice Chief of the Army Staff.

title: ‘It isn’t over yet’: How Tamil Nadu’s power battle turned into one hell of a ride
content: Following the Tamil Nadu election results, AIADMK factions engaged in backroom dealings to either support or block C Joseph Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam from forming a government. While some AIADMK leaders attempted to engineer defections for TVK, Edappadi K Palaniswami opposed any support, leading to MLAs being moved to resorts.

title: Ex-intern alleges harassment by company CEO, claims he threatened to ruin her career
content: Delhi Police have filed an FIR against a south Delhi private company's CEO accused of sexual harassment and intimidation by a former 20-year-old intern. The intern alleged inappropriate late-night calls, unwanted touching, and threats to her career. The company director and HR personnel are also accused of harassment, with the intern reportedly asked to leave early and denied documents.

title: At 87%, Bengaluru tops Indian cities in dowry cases, says NCRB report
content: Bengaluru leads the nation in dowry prohibition cases, accounting for 87% of metropolitan city incidents with 878 registered. The city also recorded 25 dowry-related deaths and the highest number of crimes against women under special and local laws. Cruelty against wives by husbands or in-laws also saw a significant number of cases in Karnataka.

title: After Gukesh's viral 'first pump' celebration, Sindarov gets revenge - Watch
content: Javokhir Sindarov defeated D Gukesh in their first-ever blitz clash at the Grand Chess Tour in Warsaw, avenging his earlier rapid loss to the World Champion. Gukesh’s rare fist-pump celebration after that previous win had gone viral. With one win each, their growing rivalry now heads into a decisive final reverse-blitz showdown before this year’s World Championship clash.

title: BJP’s 1st, Bengal’s 9th: Suvendu to take oath as CM today in PM Modi-Amit Shah presence
content: Suvendu Adhikari has been unanimously elected BJP legislative party leader and will be Bengal's next chief minister. Union Home Minister Amit Shah made the announcement, highlighting Adhikari's fighting spirit and administrative experience. The swearing-in ceremony is scheduled for Saturday, with Prime Minister Narendra Modi in attendance.

title: Allen scripts T20 history, becomes first-ever batter in NZ's history to...
content: Finn Allen smashed an unbeaten 100 off 47 balls to help Kolkata Knight Riders beat Delhi Capitals by eight wickets in IPL 2026. The knock made Allen the first New Zealand batter to score three T20 centuries in a calendar year. KKR’s bowlers, led by Sunil Narine and Anukul Roy, earlier restricted DC to 142/8.

title: 'Currently in therapy': Simone Biles opens up on mental health journey
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: Former NFL scout links Dianna Russini to Belichick, Payton affair claims
content: The Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy intensified after a former NFL scout alleged that the veteran insider had relationships with several prominent NFL figures, including Bill Belichick and Sean Payton. The claims spread rapidly online following renewed attention from TMZ reports and viral social media discussions. Sports analyst Jason Whitlock also mocked the scandal publicly, calling it “The Love Bloat.” Despite the growing controversy, Patriots players reportedly continue supporting Vrabel ahead of the upcoming season.

title: Patrick Mahomes emotional as Brittany Mahomes earns major women’s sports honor
content: Patrick Mahomes publicly celebrated Brittany Mahomes after she received the inaugural Champion for Change Award from United WE for her impact on women’s sports. Brittany was recognized for helping build CPKC Stadium and growing the Kansas City Current into one of the NWSL’s most valuable franchises. Patrick’s emotional Instagram message to his wife quickly gained attention online.

title: After three Lok Bhavan visits, Vijay is one VCK step away from CM chair
content: TVK had the support of 117 legislators in the 234-member assembly, as VCK was yet to give its support in letter

title: IPL 2026 playoff qualification scenarios: What DC need after KKR result
content: Delhi Capitals' playoff hopes are in dire straits after an eight-wicket loss to Kolkata Knight Riders. A batting collapse against KKR's spinners, coupled with a poor Net Run Rate, has pushed DC to eighth place. They now need to win all three remaining matches to have any chance of qualification, facing a must-win scenario in every upcoming fixture. Unless Delhi suddenly produce their most complete cricket of the season over the final three matches, IPL 2026 is rapidly heading towards an early finish for Axar Patel’s side.

title: Krunal Pandya Interview: ‘Nothing bigger than playing for the country'
content: Krunal Pandya reflects on his 11-season IPL journey, crediting adaptability, fitness and constant reinvention for his longevity and success with Royal Challengers Bengaluru. The left-arm spinner discusses refining his bowling for bounce and deception, embracing challenges, and staying driven by an ambition to return to India’s national team. Gratitude, resilience and evolution remain central to his career and mindset.

title: India conducts its maiden test of 'nuclear-capable ICBM' off Odisha coast
content: India's DRDO successfully test-fired a nuclear-capable intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) off the Odisha coast, a development that could place any country worldwide within its strike range. This achievement signifies India's mastery of complex rocket propulsion and guidance systems, enhancing its strategic deterrence capabilities.

title: US imposes sanctions on 10 individuals, companies for aiding Iran's weapons sector
content: The US has sanctioned ten individuals and companies, including those in China and Hong Kong, for aiding Iran's drone and ballistic missile programs. These measures aim to curb Iran's military capabilities and its threat to regional shipping. The sanctions target entities involved in procuring weapons components and raw materials, with a warning to foreign firms engaged in illicit Iranian trade.

title: Catch-22 for visa applicants as US adds 2 more questions
content: US non-immigrant visa applicants now face new questions about experiencing or fearing harm in their home countries, with affirmative answers potentially leading to denial. This change could impact future asylum claims, as applicants might be challenged on the veracity of their statements. Immigration experts suggest these questions target legal immigrants who might later seek asylum.

title: Explainer: Can hantavirus outbreak become Covid 2.0?
content: Health authorities are urgently tracing passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship following a hantavirus outbreak that has claimed three lives. The Andes strain, capable of human-to-human transmission, was identified, prompting isolation and contact tracing. While not comparable to COVID-19, the virus requires close contact to spread.

title: Why Bombay HC upheld 2018 acquittal in 2005 fake encounter case of Sohrabuddin Shaikh: Explained
content: The Bombay High Court upheld a trial court's acquittal of 21 police officers and others in the Sohrabuddin Shaikh, Kausar Bi, and Tulsiram Prajapati alleged fake encounter case. The court found the prosecution failed to establish abduction, conspiracy, or murder, deeming the evidence insufficient to overturn the initial verdict. Appeals by Shaikh's brothers were dismissed.

title: 'Suspension of all kinetic activity': Trump announces 3-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire
content: US President Trump announced a three-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire from May 9-11, hoping for a long-term peace deal and a prisoner exchange. Despite this, fighting persisted, with both sides reporting attacks. Ukraine accused Russia of continuing assaults, while Russia claimed to intercept numerous drones. The truce follows Russia's Victory Day, a proposal Kyiv had previously dismissed.

title: Not 'actually tracking' anyone: Rs 7,000 GPS watches for Haj pilgrims stops working
content: Indian Haj pilgrims are questioning a mandatory Rs 7,000 GPS smartwatch, part of a Rs 10,000 airfare hike. Many devices, procured by the Haj Committee and supplied by Sekyo Innovations, reportedly fail to turn on or track pilgrims, causing distress, especially for the elderly. This initiative, aimed at safety, faces criticism over cost and execution.

title: Supreme Court in no mood to condone Madhya Pradesh minister in Col Sofiya Qureshi case
content: The Supreme Court expressed strong disapproval of Madhya Pradesh minister Kunwar Vijay Shah's controversial remarks, questioning the state government's decision on prosecuting him. Despite the Solicitor General suggesting the minister intended praise, the bench noted a lack of repentance and the statement's unfortunate impact. The court also directed the SIT to investigate other instances of the minister's objectionable statements.

title: We do not express opinion on correctness of arrest or non-arrest: SC
content: The Supreme Court emphasized its reluctance to direct arrests, prioritizing evidence collection and witness protection. While monitoring investigations, the bench declined to comment on the merits of the Anil Ambani Group (ADAG) case, citing past collateral damage to reputations. The CBI has filed nine FIRs totaling Rs 27,337 crore against ADAG entities.

title: India gets new labour law as Centre notifies wage code, caps work week at 48 hours
content: New labour codes have capped weekly working hours at 48, with overtime paid at double the hourly wage. Final rules for wages and industrial relations codes are out, paving the way for implementation. A National Reskilling Fund will support retrenched workers with new skills, financed by employers.

title: Satheesan backers stage protests, Kerala Congress leadership steps in
content: Congress leadership intervened as supporters of opposition leader V D Satheesan held demonstrations across Kerala, demanding his appointment as the next chief minister. Some activists allegedly tore down posters of AICC leader K C Venugopal, also a contender for the post, prompting the KPCC to direct workers to refrain from such actions.

title: ED raids Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora, linked persons again
content: The action was taken under the criminal sections of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), the officials said

title: BJP’s victory in Bengal will plug a big hole in national security, says Amit Shah
content: The victory in West Bengal is significant because after 100 years of ideological continuity, we have a government from every state from Gangotri to Gangasagar, says Shah at meeting of legislators in Kolkata

title: Three arrested for organ trade in Kerala
content: The two local agents held in Kollam were responsible for scouting vulnerable donors and managing the fraudulent paperwork required to facilitate the illegal transplants

title: Kerala filmmaker John Abraham’s cult classic Amma Ariyan’s restored version to premiere at Cannes
content: The film with a cult following, restored by the not-for-profit organisation Film Heritage Foundation, is the only Indian feature film selected for world premiere at the festival this year. The premiere is scheduled on May 16

title: Tiger ‘Mangala’ relocated to Thrissur Zoological Park
content: The big cat, which suffers from bilateral cataracts, had been undergoing specialised care by the Forest department for the past six years

title: Electricity consumption dips as summer rain gives Kerala some respite from heat
content: Daily consumption levels down by 10 mu or more compared to many days in April. However, storage in he hydel reservoirs of KSEB has not seen a marked improvement

title: Plea in Supreme Court says Governor is ‘duty-bound’ to invite TVK to form government in Tamil Nadu
content: The petition, filed by advocate A. Lakshminarayanan, said such a move by the Governor would “sound the death-knell for democracy” and be against the mandate of the electorate

title: Stone by stone, Hanamkonda’s Kakatiya-era dance pavilion rises again
content: After a 42-year conservation effort, the Archaeological Survey of India has rebuilt the sunken dance pavilion of Hanamkonda’s Thousand Pillar Temple using Kakatiya-era sandbox technology. The reconstruction of the temple’s iconic Nandi sculpture, brings back the grandeur of the 12th century monument

title: Tourism and stress hormones: The troubling reality inside tiger habitats
content: A multi-reserve study by Hyderabad-based CCMB has found that tigers living near tourism roads and human activity experience higher stress levels, with core-zone tigers reacting more sharply than those in buffer areas. Researchers warn that unmanaged disturbance could affect breeding success and cub development, prompting calls for science-based tourism regulation

title: Bangladesh failed to give ‘actionable response’ to diplomatic notes: India
content: In diplomatic note on April 30, New Delhi says it sent over 1,000 messages to Dhaka on repatriation of illegal immigrants since 2020, but the neighbouring country has not extended necessary cooperation

title: West Bengal oath-taking LIVE: Suvendu Adhikari to take oath as first BJP CM today
content: The BJP registered a historic victory in the just-held Assembly elections, securing 207 of the 293 seats, ending the TMC’s 15-year rule and dealing Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: Vijay’s TVK close to majority mark | A timeline of events
content: Following inconclusive Assembly elections in Tamil Nadu that led to an unprecedented hung assembly in the State, government formation has been delayed amidst coalition discussions. Here is a timeline of events.

title: Uniforms, ID cards mandatory for power department staff across Karnataka
content: Decision follows a representation submitted to the Energy Minister highlighting rising incidents of impersonation and public concern over fake electricity department staff.

title: Suvendu Adhikari set to take oath as the first BJP Chief Minister of West Bengal on May 9
content: Chief Minister-elect Suvendu Adhikari reached Kolkata Lok Bhavan and met Governor R.N. Ravi to stake claim to form the government. He will take oath as the Chief Minister of West Bengal at Kolkata’s Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday

title: Government appoints Lt Gen NS Raja Subramani (Retd) as next Chief of Defence Staff
content: Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani is currently Military Adviser, National Security Council Secretariat from September 1, 2025

title: VCK to support TVK, announcement soon
content: Sources said VCK Legislature Party leader Vanniarasu is on his way to Chennai to sign the letter of support

title: Government appoints Vice Admiral Krishna Swaminathan as next Chief of Naval Staff
content: The present Chief of the Naval Staff Admiral Dinesh Kumar Tripathi, PVSM, AVSM, NM will retire from service on May 31, 2026

title: Killers of Suvendu’s aide still at large; post-poll violence continues in Bengal
content: While forensics collected samples from the crime scene, it did not find any fingerprints in the car used by the assassins; a team has gone to Uttar Pradesh to trace connections of the killers there

title: Tamil Nadu Congress MLAs camping in Hyderabad amid uncertainty in government formation
content: The five MLAs reportedly arrived on Thursday (May 7, 2026) and planned to leave after reports came in that Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Arlekar had asked Mr. Vijay to form the government

title: SC/ST representation beyond reserved seats still remains limited, show T.N. Assembly election results
content: Barring the AIADMK front, the other 3 fronts fielded SC/ST candidates in general constituencies. In such contests, the lone MLA elect was P. Viswanathan of the Congress, who won from Melur

title: Final decision on Kerala CM turns into political cliff-hanger as ball now in Congress High Command’s court
content: The question of who among the three probables – V.D. Satheesan, K.C. Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala – will make the cut appears set to go down to the wire, with High Command reportedly scheduled to make pivotal announcement on May 10

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: Left backs TVK but VCK undecided as uncertainty continues
content: Vijay meets Governor for the third time with tally of 116, two short of majority; AMMK’s Dhinakaran alleges horse-trading; Left leaders cite need to form a popularly elected government by May 10 to prevent the imposition of President’s Rule

title: SC criticises Punjab govt. for inability to curb drug menace
content: Drug abuse and trafficking continue galore as only small peddlers were caught while none dared to lay a hand on the big fish who ran the racket, says CJI-led Bench

title: Only Congress party can defeat BJP in the battle of ideologies, says Rahul Gandhi
content: Rahul Gandhi joins Haryana Congress leader’s ‘Sadbhav Yatra’, accuses BJP of attacking democratic system and Ambedkar’s Constitution

title: MGNREGS coverage and workdays fell sharply in 2025–26: LibTech India
content: Despite a rise in registered households, employment generation dropped across most States, leading  to an average income loss of ₹1,221 for each household; 40% fewer households completed 100 days of work

title: Behind the insistence on 'majority support' looms the shadow of President's Rule
content: A Governor's first priority is to ensure the formation of a stable government, the Supreme Court says

title: Lakhimpur Kheri case: Supreme Court expresses disappointment over non-production of witnesses
content: It asked the trial judge to make an endeavour to conclude the trial in a time-bound manner, and also to file a status report before it

title: Wet spell and easterlies likely to bring relief from peak summer heat in State for a week
content: According to the RMC, many places over South T.N. and a few places in the northern districts are likely to receive light to moderate rain over the weekend; prevailing weather systems will also bring light to moderate rainfall in some areas of Chennai on Saturday and reduce day temperature

title: BJP calls Sanjay Raut 'joker of Indian politics' over Donald Trump’s post on West Bengal polls
content: BJP spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla alleged that SS-UBT MP Sanjay seems to “besmirch India's image for domestic political petty battles” and calls him the “joker of Indian politics"

title: GBA likely to seek State government’s intervention to postpone elections
content: The move comes after Karnataka State Election Commissioner G.S. Sangreshi, following a meeting with senior GBA officials on Friday, said the government had been asked to identify feasible dates for conducting the elections within the June 14 to June 24 window

title: Administration has taken precautionary measures after identifying disaster-prone areas in Dakshina Kannada: DC
content: Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao directs officials to make frequent visits to disaster-prone areas and take immediate precautionary measures

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: AMMK chief Dhinakaran refutes party's support to TVK
content: Dhinakaran accused the TVK of submitting a "forged" letter to the Governor and slammed them for horse trading; TVK has reportedly denied these charges and accused Mr. Dhinakaran of spreading "false news"

title: Mirwaiz expresses concern over closure of school-cum-seminary under UAPA in Kashmir
content: “The banning of the Jamia Siraj Ul Uloom school under the stringent Unlawful Activities Prevention Act (UAPA) is a serious matter. It has stoked apprehension among people that our religious identity is being targeted,” said the Mirwaiz

title: Panel lawyers appointed for bonded labour cases in Thanjavur
content: Seven lawyers in Thanjavur were appointed partly due to the volume of cases and partly because they had undergone specialised training imparted by the International Justice Mission

title: Punjab worse than West Bengal under AAP rule, says Haryana CM Saini
content: Taking on the AAP dispensation in Punjab, Nayab Singh Saini alleged that they are busy in corruption and people are fed up while industrialists are shifting

title: Rat poison in watermelon killed four of a family in Mumbai: forensic report
content: Further probe is being conducted by the JJ Marg police station, which has recorded statements of kin and neighbours

title: ‘Want to live in India? sing Vande Mataram’, says Haryana Minister Anil Vij
content: “Vande Mataram is our national song and holds the same importance as the national anthem. Singing and respecting it is just as essential as respecting Jana Gana Mana If one wants to live in India, one must sing Vande Mataram,” Mr. Vij said

title: Bombay High Court rejects bail plea of accused in Baba Siddiqui murder case
content: Chetan Paradhi, in his bail application, said he was being falsely implicated. He argued that the charge-sheet had no material to show he was a member of the syndicate or had committed the crime

title: Nitish and BJP have no moral right to speak on dynastic politics, says Tejashwi Yadav
content: Tejashwi Yadav pointed out that without contesting any elections or holding membership in any house, Nitish Kumar’s son, Nishant, and Upendra Kushwaha’s son, Deepak Prakash, have been appointed Ministers

title: Jabalpur cruise boat tragedy: Nightmare on the Narmada
content: Of the 41 passengers and crew members onboard a cruise vessel on the Narmada River, 13 died when it capsized during a violent storm in the Bargi Dam reservoir on April 30. The tourism department-operated cruise had been carrying families seeking respite from the summer heat. Mehul Malpani reports on the tragedy in Jabalpur and the questions it has raised over safety protocols and preparedness for inland vessel operations

title: Congress slams Modi Government over women’s safety citing NCRB data
content: Mallikarjun Kharge said NCRB data pointed that cybercrime had surged by 1,689% and 10,546 farmers, 52,931 daily wage labourers and 14,488 students died by suicide in 2024

title: CBI files charge sheet against Shubhkamna Buildtech and others
content: The CBI is currently investigating 50 cases registered pursuant to the directions of the Supreme Court against various builder companies and financial institutions across the country in matters concerning cheating and diversion of funds involving homebuyers

title: Inside Sundar Pichai’s $40m California mansion with an infinity pool
content: The TOI Lifestyle Desk is a dynamic team of dedicated journalists who, with unwavering passion and commitment, sift through the pulse of the nation to curate a vibrant tapestry of lifestyle news for The Times of India readers. title: Over 100 Sick With 'Food Poisoning' After Eating Sweets At Rajasthan Wedding
content: The sweets, including rasmalai, were prepared for the function on Thursday night and were distributed to locals on Friday.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Trump Expects Iran Reply 'Tonight' On Latest US Proposal
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: Vice Admiral Krishna Swaminathan, Who Commanded INS Vikramaditya, Named New Navy Chief
content: Vice Admiral Krishna Swaminathan has commanded missile vessels INS Vidyut and INS Vinash, the missile corvette INS Kulish, the guided missile destroyer INS Mysore, and the aircraft carrier INS Vikramaditya.

title: India Appoints New Defence, Navy Chiefs, To Assume Office By Month-End
content: Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani To Be New Chief Of Defence Staff, Will Take Over From General Anil Chauhan

title: West Bengal Government Formation Highlights: Suvendu Adhikari Named Next Chief Minister Of Bengal
content: West Bengal Government Formation LIVE Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Union Home Minister Amit Shah, BJP president Nitin Nabin, along with several Union ministers, are expected to attend the swearing-in ceremony.

title: Meet Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani, India's New Chief Of Defence Staff
content: Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani is currently serving as the Military Adviser at the National Security Council Secretariat, a position he has held since September 1, 2025.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE: AMMK Files Complaint Against Vijay's TVK Over "Forged" MLA Support Letter
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: There is buzz that the Dravidian arch-rivals DMK and AIADMK may come together after over 50 years to form a government and keep Vijay out of power.

title: Soldier To General: Suvendu Adhikari's Oath As Bengal Chief Minister Today
content: BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari is set to become West Bengal's first chief minister from the party today, after being unanimously elected leader of the BJP legislature party. Home Minister Amit Shah made the announcement on Friday.

title: TVK Claims MLA Kamaraj Wrote Letter In Support, Refutes AMMK Chief's Remarks
content: The party, which won 108 seats in the polls and fell short of the majority mark of 118 seats, asserted that it did not need to "bargain or negotiate" with anyone for support.

title: Opinion: Blog | Suvendu Adhikari: The Man Who Never Forgot How He Was Denied A Stage In 2011
content: The story of Suvendu Adhikari begins in 2011 - from a stage in Dharmatala that made him invisible and denied him the limelight he thought should've been his.

title: 'Could Be Forgery': AMMK Chief To NDTV On TVK Claiming Support Of His MLA
content: TTV Dhinakaran said he and his party's MLA, Kamraj, have signed the letter of support to the AIADMK and submitted it to the Governor.

title: Satellite Images Show Oil Slick Off Iran's Kharg Island: Report
content: According to the satellite images, a massive oil slick is spreading near Iran's Kharg Island--a largest oil terminal, oil pipelines, storage tanks of Islamic Republic.

title: AMMK Alleges "Horse Trading" After MLA Goes Missing Amid Tamil Nadu Drama
content: Vijay's party TVK secured letters of support from the Congress, CPI and CPI(M), with the two Left parties extending outside support.

title: Google Settles Racial Discrimination Lawsuit For $50 Million
content: The suit claimed the company steered them into lower-level and lower-paid jobs and subjected them to a hostile work environment if they speak out.

title: 3-Year-Old Girl Raped By School Staff In Delhi; Accused Granted Bail
content: A three-year-old girl was allegedly raped by a 57-year-old staff member inside a private school in west Delhi's Janakpuri area, police said on Friday.

title: Did SIR Impact West Bengal Results? What Election Commission Data Says
content: The numbers suggest that while there were indeed large-scale deletions of "ineligible" electors during the SIR process, there were also equally significant additions.

title: "No Repentance": Top Court On Minister's Colonel Sofiya Qureshi Remark
content: A bench of Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi observed that he should have apologised immediately over his remarks referring to Colonel Qureshi as a "sister of terrorists".

title: In Tamil Nadu Political Chaos, A 1997 Pramod Mahajan Speech Finds Relevance
content: The speech, delivered on April 11, 1997, during discussions on the survival of Prime Minister HD Deve Gowda's United Front government, is remembered not just for its sharp observation but also for its humour.

title: Marco Rubio Says US Ties With Church Can Withstand Trump-Pope Tensions
content: Marco Rubio, who visited the Vatican on Thursday, told reporters on Friday before leaving Rome the meeting was "very positive".
